name: La Parilla Norteno-Style Arbol Chile Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1/8 teaspoon Mexican oregano, toasted and ground
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the corn oil in a medium-sized skillet over medium-high heat until hot but not smoking.
2. Fry the chiles, 1 or 2 at a time, until puffed and brown, about 10 seconds. Do not burn; they will taste bitter.
3. Shake off excess oil from the chiles and place them in a food processor.
4. Put the tomatillos in a small saucepan, cover with water, and place over high heat. Bring to a boil, lower the heat to a simmer, and cook until tender, about 10 minutes. The tomatillos will have changed color and be soft but still whole.
5. Add the tomatillos to the food processor along with the garlic. Process until finely chopped.
6. Add the cilantro and water and continue to process until smooth.
7. Add the onion, salt, oregano, and cumin and pulse to mix.
8. Store tightly covered in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
